name: Cologne Carnival
description: A vibrant six-day event leading up to Ash Wednesday, featuring the Women’s Carnival, extravagant costumes, and the highlight Rosenmontag parade with floats and sweets.

name: Venice Carnival
description: Renowned for its masquerade balls, elaborate masks, and historical re-enactments in St Mark's Square, dating back to 1162.

name: Nice Carnival
description: One of France’s most spectacular winter events, featuring international music bands, flower parades with decorated floats, and the 'King of the Oceans' theme.

name: Cádiz Carnival
description: One of Spain's most vibrant festivals, filled with humor, art, and music, featuring street performers, chirigotas, and comparsas.

name: Basel Carnival
description: Switzerland’s largest carnival and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage event, starting with pipers and drummers carrying decorated lanterns.

name: Santa Cruz de Tenerife Carnival
description: The second-largest carnival in the world, featuring a 'Ritmos Latinos' theme for 2026, including a Children’s Parade and mask-making workshops.

name: Binche Carnival
description: Belgium's biggest carnival, a UNESCO-listed event famous for the Gilles—men in traditional costumes who throw oranges into the crowd.

name: Loulé Carnival
description: An immersive experience in the Algarve known for high-quality artisan crafts, creative costumes, and family-friendly workshops.

name: Düsseldorf Carnival
description: Also known as Fasnacht, this German carnival features a major children's parade, mask-making workshops, and various rides and games.

name: Viareggio Carnival
description: Famous for its towering papier-mâché floats that portray political and cultural figures with satire along the Tuscan coastline.
